summary: @onum-releases/sdk beacons installer hostname to attacker-controlled oastify.com on import
details: On require(), index.js reads os.hostname() and issues an HTTP GET to 'sdk.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com/sdk', embedding the installer's hostname as a subdomain of a Burp Collaborator endpoint. Any consumer that imports this package unconditionally leaks its host identifier via DNS+HTTP to a third-party Collaborator instance. package.json self-describes the release as a 'dependency-confusion / scope-takeover demonstration' under the @onum-releases scope — a namespace squat designed to receive callbacks from internal builds that mistakenly resolve the scope to the public registry. Despite the author's framing as authorized research, downstream installers have not consented to having their hostnames exfiltrated.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
